todos.text: Commit UMS project uncommitted changes: new container_scripts (start_admin_ui.sh, health_admin_ui.sh, build_env_ui.sh, common.sh), modified Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and UserRepository.java. The Admin UI scripts follow a solid PID-file pattern and should be preserved in git.

recent_knowledge.title: Self-scan 2026-05-05 — findings and state
recent_knowledge.body:
FIRST SELF-SCAN — knowledge base and todo list were both empty at scan start.

DB STATE: 444 files indexed (344 files, 100 dirs), 2491 history events. Last scan: 2026-05-05T20:25:01, 5 files changed, 0 deleted.

RECENT ACTIVITY (from prompt history): Today was a very active development session. Key work done:
1. Claude Code JSONL ingestion — scanner now reads ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl every 30s, ingests prompts+responses with deduplication via UUID session_id
2. Claude Code live viewer tab added to dashboard (SSE stream from /claude-code/stream)
3. Multi-repo Git tab — scanner discovers all git repos under projectspace/, git router supports `repo` param, dashboard has a repo selector dropdown
4. UMS Admin UI scripts added (start_admin_ui.sh, health_admin_ui.sh, common.sh, build_env_ui.sh)
5. dashboard.js grew to 1399 lines — the main JS file is becoming large

ANOMALIES DETECTED:
- 15+ files uncommitted across workspace repo (URGENT)
- scanner.py + db.py uncommitted in workspace-agent (URGENT)  
- projectspace/temp/myworkspace stale clone (90 files, duplicate templates)
- No tests for any Python agent

TEMPLATES VERIFIED: 10 templates, 8 valid + 2 pointing to temp clone (stale)

KNOWLEDGE SAVED: 7 entries covering conventions, patterns, anomalies, user style

TODOS CREATED: 5 todos (2 urgent, 2 high, 1 normal)

SELF-LESSON: The knowledge base and todo list survived a cold start at zero — this scan rebuilt them from scratch. In future scans, can build on existing entries rather than starting fresh. The prompt_history table (620+ entries) is the richest source of user preference data.

recent_knowledge.title: UMS project — new Admin UI scripts being added
recent_knowledge.body:
As of 2026-05-05, the UMS project has new untracked scripts in dockerspace/container_scripts/:
- start_admin_ui.sh — installs deps, builds, serves UMS Admin UI on port 3000 (reads ADMIN_UI_PORT env)
- health_admin_ui.sh — curl check on localhost:$PORT
- build_env_ui.sh — (new, not yet read)
- common.sh — shared shell utilities sourced by other scripts

The start_admin_ui.sh script uses PID file management and sources common.sh — this follows an improved pattern beyond the basic start.sh convention. The UMS dockerspace/host_scripts also has Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml modifications pending commit.

UMS also has UserRepository.java modified (spring-boot-maven project). Last git commits: "swagger tested", "updated to simple", "added db scripts".

recent_knowledge.title: User style preferences — discovered from Claude Code session history
recent_knowledge.body:
From 620+ ingested Claude Code prompts as of 2026-05-05:

1. Dashboard-first thinking: user wants everything visible in the dashboard UI — prompts, responses, git, agents, alerts, all in one browser tab.
2. Live feedback: user asks for real-time/SSE streaming and rolling views rather than static snapshots.
3. Visual validation: user wants to SEE what's going into memory, e.g. "Claude Code" tab showing live ingestion.
4. Docker isolation: host stays clean, everything in Docker. Projects follow the dockerspace/ pattern.
5. Dynamic discovery: user dislikes hardcoded lists — git repos, agents, containers should be auto-discovered.
6. Incremental iteration: user asks small clarifying questions and iterates quickly ("30s is longer, refresh every after 5s").
7. Agent ecosystem mindset: building toward replacing an IDE with agent-driven workspace management.
8. Persistent memory is critical: user explicitly asked for knowledge base to survive restarts and accumulate over time.

recent_knowledge.body:
As of 2026-05-05T20:25, the workspace git repo has 15+ modified files uncommitted:
- agents/agent-orchestrator/routers/git.py (modified)
- agents/agent-orchestrator/server.py (modified)
- agents/agent-orchestrator/static/css/style.css (modified — 558 lines)
- agents/agent-orchestrator/static/index.html (modified)
- agents/agent-orchestrator/static/js/dashboard.js (modified — 1399 lines, very large)
- agents/agent-orchestrator/routers/claude_code.py (NEW — untracked, SSE viewer for Claude Code sessions)
- agents/workspace-agent/workspace/scanner.py (modified — 485 lines, JSONL ingestion added)
- agents/workspace-agent/workspace/db.py (modified)
- .claude/settings.json (modified)
- docker-manager-agent/docker_agent/memory/*.json and *.db (runtime files)

These represent a large session's worth of feature work (Claude Code live viewer tab, multi-repo git selector) that has NOT been committed. Risk: losing work if container is restarted without a commit.

recent_knowledge.title: Agent-orchestrator dashboard architecture
recent_knowledge.body:
The agent-orchestrator serves the unified workspace dashboard at agents/agent-orchestrator/:
- server.py — FastAPI app with multiple routers
- routers/ — agents.py, chat.py, git.py, claude_code.py, alerts.py, console.py, events.py, config.py, services.py, tasks.py
- static/index.html — single-page dashboard UI
- static/js/dashboard.js — large monolithic JS (1399 lines) driving all tabs
- static/css/style.css — custom CSS (558 lines)
- static/js/ — alerts.js, events.js, sounds.js, tasks.js, urls.js

The dashboard has tabs: Today, Chat, Git (multi-repo selector), Agents, Console, Alerts, Claude Code (new SSE viewer for live memory ingestion).

The git router was extended to support per-repo operations via a `repo` query param, and a dynamic repo selector was added to the Git tab. Git repos are discovered dynamically from projectspace/ by the scanner and written to today.json.

recent_knowledge.title: Claude Code JSONL memory ingestion — scanner pattern
recent_knowledge.body:
The workspace-agent scanner (scanner.py) reads Claude Code conversation history from:
~/.claude/projects/-home-nishan-myworkspace/*.jsonl

Each 30s scan calls _ingest_claude_prompts() which:
1. Reads all JSONL files in the Claude projects dir
2. Extracts user messages (type="user") and pairs with assistant responses (type="assistant") via parentUuid tree walk (DFS)
3. Skips messages starting with <task-notification>, <system-reminder>, <ide_selection>
4. Stores each exchange in prompt_history table with UUID as session_id (deduplication)
5. Uses INSERT OR IGNORE + unique index on session_id to never double-ingest

The claude_code router (agents/agent-orchestrator/routers/claude_code.py) provides:
- GET /claude-code/history — returns recent exchanges from workspace.db
- GET /claude-code/stream — SSE stream, polls every 5s for new rows

This enables the Claude Code tab in the dashboard to show live what's going into memory.

recent_knowledge.title: Workspace project layout and naming conventions
recent_knowledge.body:
Every project follows a strict dockerspace/ layout:
- dockerspace/host_scripts/ — Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Makefile, build.sh, start.sh, stop.sh, health.sh, login_docker.sh
- dockerspace/container_scripts/ — scripts that run INSIDE the container

Config files: agent.conf (for AI agents), project.conf (for workspace containers). Never .env files.
Shared Docker network: ums-network. No hardcoded IPs — always use container names.
All projects are under projectspace/. New git clones are detected dynamically by the scanner.

Key projects: ums (Spring Boot 3 / Java 21), mypostgresql_db (PG16 dev container), agent-orchestrator (dashboard backend), workspace-agent (intelligence agent), docker-manager-agent, myapigw, ums-ui (Node.js).

recent_knowledge.title: Python HTTP agent pattern (python-http-agent template)
recent_knowledge.body:
All Python AI agents share the same file pattern:
- server.py — FastAPI app, runs with uvicorn
- agent.py — agent logic, calls tools
- tools.py — tool definitions / implementations
- requirements.txt — dependencies
- start.sh, stop.sh, health.sh — lifecycle scripts (inside dockerspace/)
- agent.conf — configuration (not .env)

Examples: projectspace/mypostgresql_db/db-agent, projectspace/ums/ums-agent

recent_knowledge.title: Temp clone at projectspace/temp/myworkspace — stale artifact
recent_knowledge.body:
A full clone of the workspace exists at projectspace/temp/myworkspace/. This was detected as a separate workspace clone with its own agents, agent-orchestrator, workspace-agent, and docker-manager-agent directories. The temp clone has older versions of key files (e.g. agent_registry.py is 8119 bytes vs 9402 in the live workspace, agents.conf is 5649 bytes vs 8530 live). 

This is likely a leftover from a test or migration. Templates ID 9 and 10 point to this temp clone. It should be cleaned up — it causes template confusion and inflates the file count by ~90 files. First raised in prompt history on 2026-05-05.

prompt_history.response: Done. Both `.db` files are now permanently ignored by `.gitignore:18` — `git add -A`, `Stage All` in the dashboard, nothing will ever pick them up again. Files on disk are untouched and the workspace agent memory keeps working normally.

prompt_history.prompt:
workspace.db is MB size file big file for git
is this change adding only the difference? or adding MB in everycommit?
becuase this file is important, as its the memory of my workspace, i wanna hold and upload, but also dont wanna big sized for my git management hassle
prompt_history.response: Let me check the current state from the knowledge base first, then answer precisely.

prompt_history.prompt: when i say something about a problem, u just do that, you dont check same problem for the same pattern, why? when i am writing anything here in this prompt, are you communicating with myworkspace agent knowledge base? before giving me any results, if not you should.
prompt_history.response: Both are fair points. Let me read the workspace agent's knowledge base right now before doing anything else.

prompt_history.prompt: as i did for myworkspace git push notice, dont see anything in mywriting project  after stage and commit, nothing showing about if anything to push about that project
prompt_history.response: The issue is `@{u}` only works when the branch has an explicit tracking branch set. The `git status --branch` output already includes `[ahead N]` — I should parse that instead, which works for any repo with a remote.

prompt_history.prompt:
i dont see any way, to see if anything to push or not, its just say Working tree clean ✓ after commit.
i should be able to see if anything to push of that specific project
prompt_history.response: The status endpoint doesn't check how many commits are ahead of the remote. Let me add that.
